data.title: Oil Declines; WTI Seen Consolidating — Market Talk
data.description: Oil prices fall in Asian trading, pulling back after overnight gains. Front-month WTI crude oil futures are 0.65% lower at $64.18/bbl; front-month Brent is down 0.7% at $68.12/bbl. WTI crude is likely to consolidate around $65/bbl, after it showed a new bullish bias and has gained more than 1.6% in the short term, says StoneX's Julian Pineda in analysis. Concerns have emerged over India's crude purchases from Russia, which could face new challenges in the coming weeks, he says. "For now, buying pressure remains in place, and if this scenario persists, it could consolidate into a more significant bullish trend in the short term," he adds. He pegs WTI crude's relevant resistance level at $67/bbl while placing support at $60/bbl. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)

data.title: Philippines Producer Deflation Slows in July
data.description:
Producer prices in the Philippines fell by 0.27% year-on-year in July 2025, slowing from a downwardly revised 0.35% drop in the previous month.

This marked the third consecutive month of decline in producer prices albeit at a softer pace, mainly due to slower decreases in the costs for the manufacture of computer, electronic, and optical products (-1% vs -2.5% in June), which contributed 84.1% to the overall slower decline.

Other main contributors to the softer decline included food products (0.5% vs 0.4%) and furniture (1.4% vs 0.7%).

Meanwhile, price deflation deepened for basic metals (-1.5% vs -0.6%), electrical equipment (-4.1% vs -3.7%), and paper & paper products (-0.8% vs -0.7%).

On a monthly basis, producer prices rose by 0.1% in July, following a 0.07% increase in the preceding period.

data.title: Australia Private Credit Growth at 3-Month High
data.description:
Australia’s private sector credit rose 0.7% month-on-month in July 2025, after two consecutive 0.6% gains and marking the fastest increase since April.

The figure beat market expectations of 0.6%, driven by a sharp pickup in business credit (1.4% vs 0.5% in June).

Meanwhile, housing credit growth held steady (at 0.5%), while personal credit notably eased (0.4% vs 0.8%).

Year-on-year, private credit expanded 7.2%, up from 6.9% in the previous two months and the strongest pace since February 2023.

data.title: Thai baht/US dollar slightly weaker on Friday
data.description:
The Thai baht weakened slightly against the U.S. dollar on Friday.

data.title: Gold prices near one-month peak on soft dollar, US rate-cut hopes
data.description:
Gold prices hovered on Friday near their highest point in more than one month, as an overall softer dollar and increased expectations for a U.S. Federal Reserve interest rate cut in September lifted demand for bullion.

FUNDAMENTALS

* Spot gold held its ground at $3,413.80 per ounce, as of 0059 GMT, after hitting its highest point since July 23 on Thursday. Bullion has gained 3.6% so far this month.

* U.S. gold futures for December delivery were flat at $3,473.80.

* The dollar weakened 0.4% overnight, and was set for a monthly drop, making greenback-priced gold less expensive for other currency holders.

* Fed Governor Christopher Waller on Thursday stepped up his call for cutting short-term U.S. borrowing costs, saying he would support an interest-rate cut next month and further reductions over the next three to six months.

* Traders are anticipating an 86% chance of a 25-basis-point rate cut at the Fed's policy meeting next month, according to CME FedWatch Tool.

* Non-yielding gold typically performs well in a low-interest-rate environment.

* Investors are now awaiting the release of the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) Price Index, the preferred inflation measure of the U.S. Fed, due later in the day, for further cues on the Fed's interest rate trajectory.

* Economists polled by Reuters expect the PCE price index (USPCEY=ECI) to rise 2.6% in July, matching the climb from the prior month.

* Meanwhile, Fed Governor Lisa Cook filed a lawsuit on Thursday claiming U.S. President Donald Trump has no power to remove her from office, setting up a legal battle that could challenge long-established norms for the U.S. central bank's independence.

* Elsewhere, spot silver eased 0.1% to $39.02 per ounce, platinum fell 0.2% to $1,356.69 and palladium was steady at $1,102.75.

data.title: Australian shares fall; benchmark index eyes best August since 2009
data.description:
Australian shares declined on Friday, dragged down by financials, but the benchmark index remained set to post its best August since 2009, driven by a stellar earnings season that is drawing to a close and bets for further rate cuts.

By 0045 GMT, the S&P/ASX 200 index fell 0.3% to 8,957.10, just 97.4 points away from the record high hit on Monday.

The benchmark index has advanced 2.7% so far in August and was set for its fifth consecutive month of rise.

Gains were supported by hopes of further policy easing, after the Reserve Bank of Australia cut rates earlier this month, while the U.S. Federal Reserve is also expected to lower rates in September.

The strong performance was also driven by outsized gains from companies such as Qantas , IDP Education , Aussie Broadband , Brambles and other companies, following their strong results.

Meanwhile, heavyweight financials lost 0.8%, after rising more than 1% on Thursday. The sub-index, which drove much of the gains on the benchmark this year, was set for a more than 3% rise this month.

The Commonwealth Bank of Australia , the National Australia Bank and Westpac shed more than 1%.

Healthcare stocks retreated 0.8%, with biotech firm CSL falling 0.3%.

Bucking the trend, technology stocks rose 2.3%, tracking the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average's record close overnight.

Among individual stocks, Harvey Norman climbed as much as 14.7% to a record high on annual profit beat and flagging a strong start to fiscal 2026.

Austal soared as much as 19.6% to a record high on strong annual results.

Meanwhile, Lynas Rare Earths slipped 5% on announcing a discounted equity raise of A$750 million ($487.4 million) and posting a huge drop in annual profit.

New Zealand's benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index gained 0.2% to 12,930.24.

($1 = A$1.5389)

data.title: Shanghai Composite Index Opens Flat at 3842.82
China's Manufacturing Activity Likely Continued to Contract in August, WSJ Poll Shows — Market Talk
data.description: China's official manufacturing PMI likely stayed in contractionary territory for the fifth straight month in August, according to a poll of economists by The Wall Street Journal. The official gauge likely stood at 49.5 in August, compared with the prior month's 49.3. The index has stayed below the 50 mark that separates expansion from contraction since April, when trade tensions between Washington and Beijing intensified. Extreme weather in the summer and Beijing's recent move to curb excess capacity have weighed on factory activity, polled economists say. China's statistics bureau is scheduled to release the PMI data on Sunday. (singaporeeditors@dowjones.com)

data.title: ChiNext Price Index Opens Flat at 2828.01
Japanese Yen Steady as Traders Weigh Data
data.description:
The Japanese yen held around 147 per dollar on Friday, remaining sideways for nearly four weeks as investors digested a series of economic reports.

July data showed both industrial production and retail sales fell short of expectations, while Tokyo’s core inflation rate stayed above the Bank of Japan’s 2% target.

At the same time, the unemployment rate eased to 2.3% from 2.5% in June, pointing to continued labor market strength.

On the trade front, Japan’s chief negotiator Ryosei Akazawa canceled a planned trip to Washington this week as Tokyo works to resolve issues in its trade agreement with the US before finalizing a deal.

Meanwhile, BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda recently stated that wages are expected to rise further amid tightening labor conditions, reinforcing expectations that another rate hike is gradually coming into view.

At its July meeting, the BOJ left rates unchanged but raised inflation forecasts and adopted a more optimistic outlook on the economy.

data.title: IOI Corp. Likely to Post Stronger Earnings — Market Talk
data.description: IOI Corp. is expected to deliver stronger earnings in FY 2026, supported by higher fresh fruit bunches production and improved downstream profit, CIMB Securities analysts Ivy Ng Lee Fang and Lim Yue Jia say in a note. Higher production will be supported by more palms reaching prime age, while firm crude palm oil prices should lift the plantation segment's earnings, they note. However, refining and oleochemical prospects remain challenging due to overcapacity and high raw material costs, while IOI associate Bunge Loders Croklaan is set to post solid earnings, they say. IOI also benefits from lower direct exposure to Indonesia's regulatory risks and a stronger balance sheet compared with large-cap peers, they add. CIMB maintains a buy rating on IOI and keeps its target price at MYR4.11. Shares are 0.8% higher at MYR3.91. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)
